title: Mutellina mutellinoides
taxonomy: Mutellina mutellinoides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant is closely related to the genus Anethum and shares similarities with other species in the same family. Mutellina mutellinoides is often found growing in dry and rocky areas, where it can form dense clusters. The plant's classification has been studied extensively, and it is widely recognized in the botanical community.

intro: Mutellina mutellinoides, also known as Blue Yarrow or Shepherd's Needle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Boraginaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Mutellina mutellinoides include flavonoids, such as 7-O-β-glucosyl-3,4-dihydroxyflavone, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Mutellina mutellinoides is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
